title: kafka-skill-creator manifest (v1)
description: Declares the Kafka topics, consumer groups, cluster shape, and per-environment contexts owned by a team. The generator turns a valid manifest into a kafka-<team> skill. v1 supports SASL/SCRAM and Schema Registry basic auth only; mTLS (#64), SASL/PLAIN (#63), and OAUTHBEARER (#65) are tracked separately.

team.description: Team identifier. Drives the generated skill name (kafka-<team>). Restricted to a path-safe character set so it can be used as a single path segment without escaping.

topics.description: Topics this team owns. Becomes the topic allowlist embedded in every generated script — scripts refuse to operate on names not in this list.

consumer_groups.description: Consumer groups this team owns. Becomes the group allowlist embedded in describe-group.sh, lag.sh, and reset-offsets.sh.

cluster.description: Cluster-wide shape. Per-environment values (broker addresses, credentials) live in .env.<context> at runtime, not here.

auth.description: Authentication mechanism. v1 accepts SASL_SCRAM only; other values are rejected with a pointer to the tracking issue.

tls.description: TLS posture for client connections. 'required' means the broker terminates TLS and the client must use it; 'none' is for cleartext SASL on a trusted network. Note: 'required' here is server-auth only — mutual TLS (client certs) is tracked in #64.

schema_registry.description: Optional Schema Registry block. Omit if the team's topics aren't schema-versioned. URL is supplied per-context via the .env file.

auth.description: Schema Registry auth. v1: 'basic' (HTTP Basic over the bearer of the SR password env var) or 'none'.

contexts.description: One entry per environment (dev / staging / prod / ...). Names appear verbatim in `kafkactl --context <name>` calls and as the uppercased segment in `CONTEXTS_<NAME>_*` env vars.

name.description: Context name. Must be a valid kafkactl context identifier and must round-trip through uppercasing for env-var lookup (so no leading digits, no dots).

sasl_mechanism.description: SCRAM variant used by this environment.
